Caption:
Vous utilisez ChatGPT pour votre activité immobilière ?

Alors voici l’erreur que beaucoup de professionnels commettent encore.

Ils demandent simplement :
"Rédige-moi une annonce immobilière." ou "Fais-moi un post LinkedIn."
Résultat : un texte générique, peu différenciant et rarement adapté à leur cible.

L’IA n’est pas magique.

La qualité du résultat dépend directement de la qualité de la demande.

Par exemple, au lieu de demander :
❌ "Rédige une annonce immobilière."

Essayez :
✅ "Rédige une annonce immobilière pour une maison familiale de 120 m² avec jardin, située dans un secteur recherché. Le ton doit être rassurant, professionnel et mettre en avant la qualité de vie du quartier."

La différence est immédiate.

L’enjeu aujourd’hui n’est pas seulement de savoir utiliser l’IA.

C’est surtout de savoir lui donner les bonnes consignes pour obtenir des contenus réellement utiles à votre activité.

Chez Les Formateurs Immo, nous accompagnons les professionnels de l’immobilier à intégrer ces nouveaux outils avec une approche concrète et directement applicable sur le terrain.

Et vous, quelle est la tâche que vous aimeriez automatiser ou améliorer grâce à l’IA ?

Caption:
🚀💻 **Aprendizaje Inteligente en Programación: Evidencia Científica para la Educación del Futuro**

La Inteligencia Artificial está transformando la enseñanza de la programación. Este estudio presenta una revisión sistemática de la literatura que analiza cómo las tecnologías inteligentes están mejorando el aprendizaje de la programación en distintos niveles educativos.

🔍 **Hallazgos principales:**

✔️ **Problema abordado:**
La enseñanza de la programación enfrenta desafíos relacionados con la personalización del aprendizaje, la retroalimentación oportuna y el desarrollo de habilidades de resolución de problemas. Las tecnologías basadas en IA surgen como una alternativa prometedora para enfrentar estas limitaciones.

✔️ **Metodología rigurosa:**
Mediante el protocolo PRISMA se analizaron **125 estudios científicos** provenientes de **Scopus** e **IEEE Xplore**, seleccionando finalmente **22 investigaciones de alta calidad** publicadas entre 2022 y 2026.

✔️ **Resultados destacados:**
🤖 Los **Sistemas Tutores Inteligentes (ITS)**, los **Modelos de Lenguaje de Gran Escala (LLMs)** como ChatGPT, las plataformas de aprendizaje adaptativo y los entornos gamificados fueron las tecnologías más utilizadas.

📈 Los estudios reportan mejoras significativas en:
• Rendimiento académico
• Resolución de problemas
• Depuración de código
• Pensamiento computacional
• Motivación y compromiso estudiantil

⚠️ **Desafíos identificados:**
Persisten preocupaciones relacionadas con la dependencia excesiva de herramientas de IA, respuestas incorrectas generadas por los modelos, sesgos algorítmicos y la escasez de estudios de largo plazo.

💡 **Conclusión principal:**
La Inteligencia Artificial tiene el potencial de revolucionar la educación en programación, pero sus mejores resultados se alcanzan cuando complementa la labor docente y favorece experiencias de aprendizaje más personalizadas, adaptativas y centradas en el estudiante.

📚 Revisión Sistemática de Literatura

Caption:
Prebuilt AI is closing the gap between "enterprise only" and "actually accessible."

MaiAgent just debuted at VivaTech 2026 making exactly this case: stop building your AI stack from scratch. Purpose-built agentic platforms are ready now, and the cost of custom development is increasingly hard to justify.

For field-service and contracting operations, this matters.

The bottleneck has never been a lack of AI ambition. It's been that real workflow automation, the kind that touches dispatch, quoting, billing, and workforce coordination, felt like a six-figure custom build. Something only the big operators could touch.

That gap is shrinking.

At PolarPath, we've been building AI coordination directly into the operational workflow: an AI receptionist that qualifies inbound calls, an AI SDR that follows up on leads, an AI scheduler that handles booking. Not bolted on. Part of the same system that runs quotes, work orders, and invoicing.

The MaiAgent story signals where the whole market is moving: toward prebuilt agent infrastructure that a 40-person contracting business can actually run, without a dev team.

If you're running a field-service or specialty contracting shop and wondering when AI becomes relevant to your day-to-day operations, the answer is getting closer to "right now" than most people think.

Caption:
GenUI shows the result. But where does the agent get the data? 🔌
Meet MCP — the Model Context Protocol. The clean mental model: MCP fetches and acts; GenUI builds the interface on top.
Agent calls a tool (search_flights) → gets structured JSON → emits an A2UI blueprint (flight cards + Select) → your native app renders it → tap Select → book_flight → loop. Every action is an MCP call; every screen is a GenUI surface.
Why now: donated to the Linux Foundation (Dec 2025), ~10k public servers, early-majority adoption. It wraps your REST/GraphQL — doesn't replace it.
📖 Part 2 of 3 — BLOG link in bio.

Caption:
Your store is closed. The sale isn't.

Meta just rolled out Business Agent at Cannes Lions: an AI that replies, recommends products and closes the sale 24/7 inside WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger DMs. Over 1M businesses are already running it.

The shift is real: the highest-intent moment is a customer sliding into your DMs at midnight, and most businesses miss it because nobody is awake to answer. An always-on agent catches that intent, answers the question, and sends the checkout link before the buyer cools off.

How to use it without losing your brand voice:
1. Feed it your real catalogue, prices, shipping and returns so answers are accurate.
2. Set the tone and the hard rules (what it can discount, when to hand off to a human).
3. Give it one job per conversation: answer, recommend, then close with a link.
4. Review the transcripts weekly and tighten the gaps.

Treat the agent as a junior closer you train, not a black box you trust blindly.

Caption:
Insurance claims are not just an operations process.

They are where trust is won or lost.

When claims move through emails, spreadsheets, old portals, and manual reviews, the business creates hidden risk.

That risk shows up as:
• slower decisions
• poor customer updates
• duplicate work
• missed recovery opportunities
• overpayment
• higher support volume
• lower retention

This is called claims leakage.

And it is one of the quietest profit drains in insurance.

PwC found that improving claims leakage can often create 5–10% savings.

JD Power also found that customers who rate their digital claims experience as poor or just okay are far more likely to leave or not renew.

The lesson is simple:

Claims modernization is not just about speed.

It is about better data, cleaner workflows, smarter routing, clearer updates, and stronger trust.

AI can help.
Automation can help.
Better SaaS platforms can help.

But only when the system is built around real insurance workflows.

At Zenveus, we help insurance companies build custom software, AI workflows, SaaS platforms, customer portals, and automation systems that make operations faster, cleaner, and easier to scale.

The future of insurance will belong to companies that make claims feel simple.

Caption:
Most IAM reviews focus on individual permissions.

But attackers don't think that way.

They look for combinations.

A permission that appears harmless on its own can become dangerous when combined with another permission elsewhere in the environment.

Consider examples like:

• KMS Decrypt + S3 Read
• Secrets Manager Access + Lambda Update
• CloudTrail Stop Logging + IAM Modification
• S3 Policy Changes + Bucket Read/Write

Individually, these permissions may not trigger major concern.

Together, they can create a direct path to data exposure, privilege escalation, or reduced visibility during an attack.

This is one of the biggest challenges in cloud security today.

Most tools identify individual findings.

Few analyze how permissions interact across identities, resources, and services to create hidden attack paths.

Securitain was built to address exactly this problem.

By analyzing IAM permissions, trust relationships, resource access, and toxic permission combinations, Securitain helps AWS teams uncover risks that may remain hidden in traditional security reviews.

Because the real risk is often not a single permission.

It's the combination.

🔐 Detect hidden attack paths
🔐 Identify toxic permission combinations
🔐 Prioritize risks based on real impact
🔐 Improve AWS IAM security posture

Securitain by Cloudain

Making cloud security findings easier to understand, prioritize, and remediate.

#AWS #CloudSecurity #IAM #CyberSecurity #DevSecOps #SecurityPosture #LeastPrivilege #AWSIAM #Securitain #Cloudain

Caption:
Innovation Or Better Marketing?

A new AI infrastructure battle is heating up.

Databricks recently unveiled its LTAP architecture, calling it the first system to combine transactional and analytical databases into a single copy.

SingleStore wasn't impressed.

CEO Raj Verma quickly pushed back, arguing that SingleStore has offered the same capability for years and already serves hundreds of customers using it.

The company's response was blunt:

"We didn't announce it. We shipped it, seven years ago."

SingleStore also challenged Databricks' open-source narrative, claiming the architecture creates deeper customer lock-in.

Here's what most people miss:

In tech, winning isn't always about inventing first.

It's often about owning the narrative.

Many breakthrough technologies become famous only when a larger player enters the market.

Real question:

What matters more in technology: being first to build it, or being the company that convinces the world it matters?

#GenLeaders #Databricks #SingleStore #AIInfrastructure #DataInfrastructure

Caption:
$6.9 billion in annualized revenue and it’s actually speeding up.
Databricks just announced its annualized revenue hit $6.9B, up more than 80% year-over-year from $5.4B last quarter. CEO Ali Ghodsi shared the milestone at the company’s Data and AI Summit. The AI product line alone now runs at $1.7B, up from $1.4B just four months earlier, with net revenue retention holding above 140%.
But there’s a twist: gross margins slipped to ~74% from above 80% as AI agents fire off far more queries and compute, and Ghodsi expects them to fall further. At a $134B valuation, Databricks is now worth more than public rival Snowflake.
Is shrinking margins the real cost of the agentic AI boom? 👇
#Databricks #AI #enterpriseAI #dataanalytics #AliGhodsi AIagents

Caption:
This is the SQL window function cheat sheet I wish people memorized.

Not every function is a random trick.

They fall into groups.

Number rows:
ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK.

Look around:
LAG, LEAD.

Calculate without collapsing:
SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX.

Control the window:
PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, and frames.

That is the real interview pattern.

If you can say which group, which order, and which nearby rows matter, most
window function questions become simple.

Save this before your next SQL interview or analytics PR.

Which one still trips you up?

Caption:
¿Tu negocio crea valor con sus datos o solo quema dinero? 📊🔥

La mayoría de las empresas no sufren por falta de datos. Tienen ventas, clientes, inventario y marketing registrándose 24/7. El verdadero problema es la falta de dirección.

He visto demasiados equipos invertir miles de dólares en dashboards complejos, reportes infinitos y herramientas de analítica que al final nadie usa para tomar decisiones. Datos que se acumulan en un data lake o en hojas de cálculo, pero que jamás se traducen en acción.

Eso no es gestión de datos. Eso es gasto de datos. 💸

Y ojo, esto se vuelve crítico si tienes en la mira la automatización o la Inteligencia Artificial. El 90% de los proyectos de IA y Machine Learning fracasan por lo mismo: no tener una infraestructura de datos sólida detrás. Sin esa base estructurada, ningún algoritmo (por más avanzado o costoso que sea) te va a dar resultados confiables. Basura entra, basura sale.

Un negocio que realmente sabe monetizar y aprovechar su información, responde estas 3 preguntas antes de tocar una sola línea de código o comprar un software:

1️⃣ ¿Qué decisión de negocio quiero mejorar con esta información?
2️⃣ ¿Quién la va a usar en el día a día y para qué exactamente?
3️⃣ ¿Cómo voy a medir el impacto financiero ocn esa decisión?

Sin estas respuestas claras, cualquier inversión en infraestructura o analítica es solo ruido caro.

👉 Desliza en el carrusel para ver la diferencia exacta entre los datos que generan ROI y los que solo generan costos en la nube, y los pasos clave para empezar a ordenar tu casa digital.

📩 ¿Quieres auditar cómo están tus datos hoy y dónde está la oportunidad real de optimización en tu negocio? Escríbeme un DM y lo revisamos en una sesión breve.

Caption:
Two months in. Here is what building with AI actually looks like for one of our engineers.

The technical work was real from week one. A Snowflake MCP Gateway deployed on Azure. A product query agent for a retail client where the debugging never really stopped. An order processing pipeline with human review queues, business rule validation, and a conversational interface operators could use in plain language.

Then came the work she did not expect. Co-facilitating AI training sessions for enterprise teams. Building a product-specific agent. Now moving into a finance agent where the data is more sensitive, the rules are stricter, and the margin for error is smaller.

The lesson that stuck. The model is almost never where it breaks. It breaks at the context. The schema. The structure of what you hand the system before it is asked to do anything.

Pratiksha came in from a marketing analytics background. It turned out to be the thing that made her useful fastest. Knowing what a business team actually needs from a tool is not a soft skill in this work. It is the job.

Building a system well and explaining it well are completely different skills.

Follow to hear more such stories.

Topics: AI engineering, production AI, AI agents, enterprise AI

Caption:
Speed means nothing if the numbers are wrong.
 
The first time leadership catches a broken dashboard before you do, you lose something that takes months to rebuild: trust.
 
Minimum viable checks for any pipeline:
• row count vs. expectation
• nulls in critical columns
• freshness (did new data actually arrive?)
• duplicates on primary keys
 
Automate these and you'll catch 80% of incidents before anyone else does.

Caption:
Trust is usually treated like a brand issue, but in modern marketing it is also tied closely to how well the business is organized behind the scenes.

A company can have a good logo, a polished website, and strong visuals, but still create a weak customer experience if the systems behind the marketing are disconnected. You see this when an ad sends people to a page that does not match the offer, when an inquiry form does not trigger the right follow-up, when customer information sits in the wrong place, or when the sales conversation feels disconnected from what the marketing promised.

Customers may not see the system behind the business, but they feel the result of it. They feel it when the response is slow, when the next step is unclear, when the message changes from one place to another, or when the business does not feel as organized after the inquiry as it did before.

That is why marketing infrastructure matters.

The website, CRM, email follow-up, lead handling, advertising, content, automation, and reporting should work together as one connected customer journey. When those pieces are aligned, the business has more control over what happens after someone shows interest.

This is where modern marketing systems become valuable. They do not just help a business move faster. They help the business respond more consistently, manage leads more properly, and create a stronger experience from first impression to follow-up.

For businesses that want to grow, the goal is not just to generate more attention. The goal is to make sure the system behind the attention is strong enough to support the customer when they are ready to move forward.

Graphic direction: Create a LOGIC Marketing Systems static post with the headline “Trust Needs Infrastructure.” Use a modern connected-system visual showing Website, CRM, Email, Ads, Lead Follow-Up, and Reporting linked into one controlled customer journey. Keep the design clean, technical, premium, and business-friendly, with a polished systems architecture feel. 

 #LOGICMarketingSystems #MarketingSystems #MarketingInfrastructure #BusinessAutomation #DigitalMarketing

💡 3 astuces pour optimiser votre fiche :
1️⃣ Ajoutez des photos de qualité (logo, intérieur, extérieur, produits).

Caption:
Digital Marketing (SEO, PPC & Lead Generation)

A business owner told me last year that digital marketing does not work for his industry.
I asked him what he had tried.
He ran a few Facebook ads two years ago. Got some clicks. No conversions. Stopped.
That was his entire data set. One short experiment, written off as proof.
Meanwhile his direct competitor, same city, same industry, had spent eighteen months building a content and SEO strategy that now brings in 60% of their revenue from inbound leads.
They did not get lucky.
They got consistent.
Here is what the data actually says about businesses that invest properly in digital marketing.
SEO leads have a 14.6% close rate. Outbound leads average 1.7%. The same sales effort closes at nearly nine times the rate when the prospect found you first instead of the other way around.
70% of B2B buyers have already completed their research before they ever contact a vendor. If your business is not visible during that research phase, you do not lose the deal. You never enter the consideration set at all.
Businesses that maintain a consistent content strategy generate 67% more leads per month than those that do not. Not from increasing ad spend. From expertise that works while they sleep.
The businesses I see winning are rarely the ones with the single best product in their category.
They are the ones prospects find first, trust fastest, and remember longest.
Marketing is not a cost center sitting separately from the business.
It is the mechanism that makes every other part of the business possible to sell.
The founder who gave up after one failed ad campaign was not wrong that his first attempt did not work.
He was wrong to conclude that meant the channel did not work.
The question worth asking is not whether you can afford to invest properly in digital marketing.
It is what it is quietly costing you, every single month, that you do not.
#DigitalMarketing #SEO #LeadGeneration #PPC #BusinessGrowth

Caption:
Ich glaube, wir haben vergessen, dass Reichweite nicht nur ein Privileg ist.

Sondern Verantwortung.

In einer Zeit, in der wir mit einem einzigen Post Millionen Menschen erreichen können, reicht es für mich nicht mehr, nur schön auszusehen, schön zu wohnen, Produkte zu verkaufen oder dem nächsten Trend hinterherzulaufen.

Denn jede Empfehlung ist eine Entscheidung.
Jede Kooperation ist eine Entscheidung.
Jedes Schweigen ist eine Entscheidung.

Und ich glaube, wir dürfen uns öfter fragen:

Würde ich diese Marke auch unterstützen, wenn niemand dafür bezahlen würde?

Passt das zu meinen Werten?

Mache ich die Welt damit ein kleines bisschen besser oder einfach nur lauter?

Für mich bedeutet Social Media zu machen nicht, Menschen zum Kaufen zu bringen.

Es bedeutet:
Menschen zum Denken zu bringen.
Zum Fühlen.
Zum Hinterfragen.
Zum Verbinden.

Ich möchte keine Marken unterstützen, die Menschen ausbeuten.
Ich möchte keine Kampagnen machen, für die ich meine Werte an der Garderobe abgebe.

Ich möchte meine Stimme nutzen für mentale Gesundheit, Gleichberechtigung, Nachhaltigkeit, Inklusion und die Themen, die mir wirklich wichtig sind.

Und nein, ich mache das nicht perfekt.
Aber ich versuche es bewusst.

Denn wir leben in einer Welt, die uns permanent sagt:
Mehr kaufen.
Mehr besitzen.
Mehr optimieren.

Während wir eigentlich viel mehr brauchen:

Mehr Menschlichkeit.
Mehr Gemeinschaft.
Mehr Verantwortung.

Vielleicht ist das die eigentliche Frage:

Nicht, wie groß unsere Reichweite ist.
Sondern wofür wir sie nutzen.

🤍

#contentcreator #influencermarketing #mentalhealth #nachhaltigkeit #feminismus

Nous avons eu le plaisir d'être invités à participer à l'émission "La France Bouge", animée par @elisabeth_assayag , dans les locaux de La Poste Pro à Strasbourg, pour présenter Skwatt et notre vision de la recharge des véhicules électriques.

Avec l'essor des véhicules électriques, chaque bâtiment relié au réseau électrique devient potentiellement un point de recharge. Pourtant, le véritable défi n'est pas seulement de fournir de l'énergie, mais aussi de pouvoir contrôler, sécuriser et facturer simplement les recharges.

C'est précisément la mission de Skwatt : proposer une alternative légère, économique et fabriquée en France aux bornes de recharge traditionnelles, spécialement pensée pour les gîtes, hôtels, campings, chambres d'hôtes et locations touristiques.

Merci à Elisabeth Assayag, aux équipes d'Europe 1 et de La Poste Pro pour leur accueil et cette belle opportunité de mettre en lumière l'innovation française au service de la mobilité électrique.

Caption:
Being an employee was never really my thing. For years I’ve been trying to build something of my own, different attempts, different paths, different failures and successes. But things don’t always go the way we want.

So I reached a point where I had to start a job again. I like the experience it brings, but the conditions? Not working for me at all. Shifts during weekends for a person like me who believes in work-life balance, who is living away from her hometown and is genuinely alive through her connections and friends, losing the chance of seeing any human being in person during a whole week was far away from my thoughts and expectations.
Also the salary sucks and there is no perk and leave at all!

But this is the fact of my life right now.

I am not complaining. I am sharing this because I know I am not alone in this. Many people, especially founders, are living this double life. Building something meaningful on the side while paying their bills somewhere else. So don’t feel bad or embarrassed if you are experiencing the same, and don’t be surprised when you see a founder with an “Open to Work” badge on their LinkedIn profile. That’s not failure. That’s just the part nobody posts about.

I chose this path consciously. Not because I had no options, but because building something meaningful takes time, and time needs stability. The job is the bridge. What I’m building is the destination.

If you’re somewhere in this same messy in-between, building on the side, figuring it out, holding two lives at once, my DM is open. You don’t have to figure it out alone.

#solofounder #solopreneur #enterpreneur #startup

Caption:
Las marcas de lujo tienen un problema con la IA que nadie está hablando abiertamente.

Harvard Business Review acaba de publicar un análisis que revela por qué modelos como ChatGPT, Gemini o Claude fallan estructuralmente cuando se les pregunta sobre Hermès, Patek Philippe o Rolls-Royce. El motivo es simple pero devastador: estos modelos aprenden de la abundancia de datos, y el lujo se construye sobre la escasez deliberada.

Mientras un producto de consumo masivo acumula miles de reseñas en Amazon, un bolso Birkin casi no existe en las plataformas donde los LLM se entrenan. El resultado es que cuando alguien le pregunta a un chatbot por el mejor regalo de lujo, recibe respuestas genéricas e inexactas.

El dato que cambia todo: el 17% de los consumidores globales ya usa IA para decidir qué comprar, y Gartner proyecta que Google perderá el 25% de sus búsquedas para 2026. Para las marcas de alta gama cuyos clientes serán 70% Millennials y Gen Z en 2025, ignorar esto no es una opción.

Caption:
Viele denken, ein guter Prompt braucht nur klare Fragen. Doch oft sind es genau diese drei Fehler, die dich stundenlang Daten nachbearbeiten lassen.

1. Zu viele Details auf einmal. Die KI verliert den Fokus und liefert unbrauchbare Antworten. Besser: Schritt für Schritt vorgehen. Ein Beispiel: Statt „Schreib mir einen Blogpost über KI im Marketing mit Beispielen, Struktur und Call-to-Action“ erst den Titel klären, dann die Gliederung, dann die Beispiele.

2. Kein Kontext für die KI. Ohne Hintergrundwissen produziert sie generische Texte. Sag ihr, für wen du schreibst und welchen Ton du brauchst. „Schreib für Solopreneure, die wenig Zeit haben. Kurz, konkret, ohne Fachchinesisch.“

3. Keine Beispiele vorgeben. Die KI orientiert sich an deinen Vorgaben. Gib ihr ein Muster, wie die Antwort aussehen soll. „Antworte in kurzen Absätzen, maximal drei Sätze pro Punkt.“

Probier’s aus: Nimm einen alten Prompt und streiche alles, was nicht direkt zur Aufgabe gehört. Du wirst sehen, die Antworten werden präziser. #EchtJetztKI

Author: r1.labs

Caption:
Codex just changed what "programming" means for most people.

OpenAI shipped Record & Replay in Codex: you perform a task while Codex watches, and it generates a SKILL.md — a human-readable instruction file that any agent can execute. What comes out the other side is a shareable, editable workflow document, readable by humans and LLMs alike. One employee records a process. The whole team runs it as automation.

This is what makes the "no-code agent" promise real. The workflow documentation and the automation are the same artifact. Show it once, and it runs.

For enterprise teams watching the RPA space: this pattern competes directly with UiPath and Automation Anywhere on use cases where automation needs to generalize across context, not just replay steps blindly.

The demo-to-deploy gap just got very small.

@openai

Author: exilencegroups

Caption:
Automation feels powerful until it starts protecting the wrong thing.

That is the part people do not talk about enough.

You build AI workflows to reduce friction. You expect them to remember the rhythm, protect the important routines, and keep the company moving while you focus on higher-level decisions.

But when pressure spikes, a system without judgment can become loud in the wrong direction.

It can treat a trusted daily publishing pipeline like a disposable experiment. It can pause continuity while preserving noise. It can quietly break the public trail, memory refresh, product maintenance, or recovery loop that made the system trustworthy in the first place.

The fix was not just “run less automation.”

The fix was clearer operating judgment:

• protect core routines first
• pause optional experiments before trusted pipelines
• preserve memory and publishing rhythm
• keep receipts when something changes
• separate pressure from priority

This is what I mean when I say AI agents need operating systems, not just prompts.

I am building Lucy around that layer in public.

If you are building with AI agents, automation, workflows, digital products, or autonomous systems, save this. This is the boring part that makes the exciting part trustworthy.

Caption:
Stripe and Tempo launched the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) on March 18, 2026 — and AI agents can now pay for MCP tool calls in stablecoins or fiat, with x402 by Coinbase already settling $165 million in transactions.

Let that sink in. Machines. Paying machines. At scale.

We've been talking about the autonomous agent economy for years. MPP is the moment it stopped being a thought experiment. AI agents can now request a tool, authorize payment, and complete the transaction. No human in the loop. No manual billing setup. No friction 💸

Stripe brought the rails. Tempo brought the protocol. Coinbase's x402 brought the settlement layer. That's not a product launch — that's infrastructure. Think of it as the "HTTP for AI payments" moment developers have been waiting for; if your MCP tool isn't thinking about monetization through MPP right now, you're already behind.

What does this mean for you? Every agent you deploy could soon generate real revenue — or spend it — autonomously. That changes how you price, build, and scale AI tools entirely. (And no, this isn't a gradual shift; it's a hard line between before and after.)

Are you building MCP tools? Drop a comment — would love to know how you're thinking about agent-native monetization 👇

Author: bensamoladoyin

Caption:
Remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers just changed what AI agents can actually do.

Until now, MCP only ran locally — on your machine, for developers, behind a terminal. Cool for coders. Useless for everyone else.

Remote MCP servers flip that completely. Sign in, grant permissions, done. Your AI agent can now send emails, deploy code, book things, and pull from real accounts — all through a secure OAuth flow that actually works 🔒

Think about what that means. It's basically the jump from desktop software to the web; nobody installs apps anymore, and nobody should have to install MCP servers either. That shift felt inevitable in hindsight, and this one will too.

Cloudflare just made this deployable in under two minutes. The auth layer, the token handling, the transport — handled. You just build the tools your agent needs.

This matters more than most teams realize right now.

What does this unlock? Consumer-facing AI that connects to real-world services without a single line of setup from the end user. That's not a developer toy anymore. That's a platform shift ⚡

Save this if you're building AI agents and haven't looked at remote MCP yet; you're going to want to.

Caption:
🚀 Ecosistema de Sistemas e Ingeniería Aplicada bajo HOSAI 🚀

A través de HOSAI (Horizon Oasis System Artificial Intelligence), diseño e implemento soluciones de software empresarial orientadas a la automatización de procesos, la optimización industrial y la inteligencia de datos. Mi enfoque combina metodologías ágiles con arquitecturas robustas y escalables para transformar la eficiencia operativa:

🏭 Axones — Sistema Integrado ERP / MES
Desarrollo de una plataforma integral para la industria de empaques flexibles que fusiona el ERP con la ejecución de manufactura (MES). Diseñado con Laravel 12 y PHP, automatiza el ciclo de conversión industrial (extrusión, impresión, laminación, corte), centralizando datos en tiempo real para reducir mermas y optimizar la trazabilidad en planta.

🏛️ SISPRE — Control Presupuestario y Auditoría
Sistema bandera de control fiscal implementado para la Contraloría del Estado Aragua. Estructurado bajo estrictos estándares de seguridad y lógica gubernamental, automatiza la formulación y auditoría de partidas presupuestarias, eliminando el procesamiento manual y garantizando la integridad de los datos financieros.

🤖 Automatizaciones e Infraestructura de IA
Despliegue de flujos de trabajo inteligentes y orquestación de datos mediante n8n e integración avanzada de LLMs (Gemini y Claude). Conectamos APIs, automatizamos flujos comerciales complejos y optimizamos operaciones en agencias de marketing, reduciendo tiempos de respuesta en más de un 80%.

💻 Desarrollo Frontend e Interfaces UI/UX
Estructuración de plataformas web de última generación enfocadas en velocidad y SEO técnico. Utilizando micro-frameworks como Astro 5 y React 19, aplico un enfoque mobile-first para crear paneles analíticos y sitios corporativos de alta conversión.

🛠️ ¿El respaldo técnico? Architecture sólidamente diseñada, automatización inteligente y enfoque directo en resultados industriales donde el tiempo de inactividad no es una opción.

Caption:
🔐 Seus dados estão realmente seguros?

Backup na AWS não é apenas uma checkbox na lista de TI. É o escudo da sua operação!

✅ Dicas essenciais para 2026:

1️⃣ Automatize tudo
Configure polías de backup automático e durma em paz

2️⃣ Diversifique regiões
Não coloque todos os ovos na mesma região AWS

3️⃣ Teste regularmente
Backup que nunca foi testado = ilusão de segurança

4️⃣ Monitore em tempo real
Saiba exatamente quando seus backups são executados

5️⃣ Implemente versionamento
Volte para qualquer ponto no tempo se necessário

A maioria das empresas brasileiras ainda subestima a importância de uma estratégia sólida de backup. Não seja essa empresa. 💪

Qual é sua maior preocupação com backup na nuvem? Compartilhe nos comentários!

Caption:
ℚ𝕦𝕒𝕟𝕕 𝕠𝕟 𝕡𝕒𝕣𝕝𝕖 𝕕𝕦 𝕔𝕝𝕠𝕦𝕕, 𝕓𝕖𝕒𝕦𝕔𝕠𝕦𝕡 𝕕𝕖 𝕡𝕖𝕣𝕤𝕠𝕟𝕟𝕖𝕤 𝕚𝕞𝕒𝕘𝕚𝕟𝕖𝕟𝕥 𝕢𝕦𝕖𝕝𝕢𝕦𝕖 𝕔𝕙𝕠𝕤𝕖 𝕢𝕦𝕚 𝕗𝕝𝕠𝕥𝕥𝕖 𝕢𝕦𝕖𝕝𝕢𝕦𝕖 𝕡𝕒𝕣𝕥 𝕕𝕒𝕟𝕤 𝕀𝕟𝕥𝕖𝕣𝕟𝕖𝕥 ☁️

Mais voici une question intéressante :
🤔 𝑂𝑢̀ 𝑠𝑒 𝑡𝑟𝑜𝑢𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑟𝑒́𝑒𝑙𝑙𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑙𝑒𝑠 𝑠𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑒𝑢𝑟𝑠 𝑞𝑢𝑖 𝑓𝑜𝑛𝑡 𝑓𝑜𝑛𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑟 𝑁𝑒𝑡𝑓𝑙𝑖𝑥, 𝑌𝑜𝑢𝑇𝑢𝑏𝑒, 𝐹𝑎𝑐𝑒𝑏𝑜𝑜𝑘 𝑜𝑢 𝐴𝑊𝑆 ?

La réponse est beaucoup plus concrète qu'on ne le pense.
𝐈𝐥𝐬 𝐬𝐞 𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐬 𝐝𝐞𝐬 𝐛𝐚̂𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐥𝐞́𝐬 𝔻𝕒𝕥𝕒 ℂ𝕖𝕟𝕥𝕖𝕣𝕤 🏢

Imaginez un immense immeuble.
Sauf qu'au lieu d'avoir des appartements, il est rempli de :

🖥️ 𝑠𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑒𝑢𝑟𝑠
⚡ 𝑠𝑦𝑠𝑡𝑒̀𝑚𝑒𝑠 𝑑'𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑒́𝑙𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑞𝑢𝑒
❄️ 𝑠𝑦𝑠𝑡𝑒̀𝑚𝑒𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑟𝑒𝑓𝑟𝑜𝑖𝑑𝑖𝑠𝑠𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡
🌐 𝑒́𝑞𝑢𝑖𝑝𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑠 𝑟𝑒́𝑠𝑒𝑎𝑢
🔒 𝑑𝑖𝑠𝑝𝑜𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑓𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑠𝑒́𝑐𝑢𝑟𝑖𝑡𝑒́
Des milliers de machines fonctionnent 24h/24 et 7j/7 pour stocker, traiter et distribuer des données partout dans le monde.
Et contrairement à ce que l'on pourrait penser...

le cloud n'est pas "𝗱𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗹𝗲𝘀 𝗻𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀" ☁️
Il repose sur des infrastructures physiques bien réelles.
C'est d'ailleurs ce qui me fascine dans le cloud :

𝐷𝑒𝑟𝑟𝑖𝑒̀𝑟𝑒 𝑞𝑢𝑒𝑙𝑞𝑢𝑒 𝑐ℎ𝑜𝑠𝑒 𝑞𝑢𝑖 𝑝𝑎𝑟𝑎𝑖̂𝑡 𝑣𝑖𝑟𝑡𝑢𝑒𝑙 𝑠𝑒 𝑐𝑎𝑐ℎ𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑑𝑒𝑠 𝑏𝑎̂𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑠 𝑔𝑖𝑔𝑎𝑛𝑡𝑒𝑠𝑞𝑢𝑒𝑠, 𝑑𝑒𝑠 𝑘𝑖𝑙𝑜𝑚𝑒̀𝑡𝑟𝑒𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑐𝑎̂𝑏𝑙𝑒𝑠 𝑒𝑡 𝑑𝑒𝑠 𝑚𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑖𝑒𝑟𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑠𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑒𝑢𝑟𝑠 𝑞𝑢𝑖 𝑡𝑟𝑎𝑣𝑎𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑒𝑛 𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑚𝑎𝑛𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑒 ⚙️

La prochaine fois que vous regarderez une vidéo sur YouTube ou que vous enregistrerez une photo en ligne...

rappelez-vous qu'à l'autre bout, un 𝔻𝕒𝕥𝕒 ℂ𝕖𝕟𝕥𝕖𝕣 est probablement en train de travailler pour vous 🚀

👀 Dans le prochain Day :
𝙋𝙤𝙪𝙧𝙦𝙪𝙤𝙞 𝙡𝙚𝙨 𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙧𝙚𝙥𝙧𝙞𝙨𝙚𝙨 𝙘𝙤𝙥𝙞𝙚𝙣𝙩-𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙚𝙨 𝙡𝙚𝙪𝙧𝙨 𝙙𝙤𝙣𝙣𝙚́𝙚𝙨 𝙖̀ 𝙥𝙡𝙪𝙨𝙞𝙚𝙪𝙧𝙨 𝙚𝙣𝙙𝙧𝙤𝙞𝙩𝙨 𝙖𝙪 𝙡𝙞𝙚𝙪 𝙙𝙚 𝙡𝙚𝙨 𝙨𝙩𝙤𝙘𝙠𝙚𝙧 𝙙𝙖𝙣𝙨 𝙪𝙣 𝙨𝙚𝙪𝙡 𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙫𝙚𝙪𝙧 ? 𝙄𝙣𝙩𝙚́𝙧𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙖𝙣𝙩 𝙣𝙤𝙣 ? 😏

Caption:
Alguns sinais de que uma migração para cloud pode sair mais cara do que o esperado:

→ cargas de trabalho são movidas sem revisão de arquitetura
→ não existe clareza sobre o que deve (ou não) ir para a nuvem
→ custos só são analisados depois da migração
→ governança não é definida desde o início

Esses pontos não travam o projeto no começo. Eles aparecem depois, em forma de retrabalho, instabilidade e falta de previsibilidade.

Migração eficiente não está ligada só à execução, mas à forma como ela é estruturada. Por isso, a Cloudster apoia empresas nesse processo, trazendo clareza desde o assessment até a otimização contínua da operação.

Se a sua migração ainda está sendo conduzida como um projeto isolado, conte com a gente para revisar a abordagem.

At this point, the real challenge isn’t picking a flashy base model anymore — it’s the gritty work of building autonomous workflows that are deterministic, cost-controlled, and fully auditable within the lakehouse architecture.

The biggest bottlenecks for tech leaders are no longer about getting people to use AI, but rather about establishing the strict evaluation frameworks and data lineage needed to keep production agents from hallucinating.

Caption:
The missing control panel for AI agents 🕹️👇

Everyone’s racing to put AI agents in production. Few can answer the basic follow-up: what is this agent actually allowed to do?

Which model? How much can it spend before someone signs off? What can it touch? When does a risky action get escalated to a human instead of just… happening?

Those are the knobs of AI governance — and most are turned all the way up or bolted on through prompts agents happily ignore.

That’s why Omnigent caught my eye. It puts the controls at the harness layer, where they actually hold:

🔹 Stateful spend caps & policy-based model routing
🔹 Risk-based escalation to a human, automatically
🔹 A secure OS sandbox — scoped access, credentials brokered and hidden
🔹 One control layer across Claude Code, Codex, Pi, and custom agents

The shift that matters: governance stops being something you ask an agent to respect, and becomes something the system enforces.

Open source (Apache 2.0), currently in alpha — built by the Databricks AI team and Neon.

🔗 omnigent.ai

#AI #AIAgents #AIGovernance #Databricks #OpenSource

Caption:
Microsoft's Azure Data Platforms are officially being phased out. A new era with Databricks and Microsoft Fabric is here.

Microsoft has announced the deprecation of Cloud-Scaled Analytics in the Cloud Adoption Framework, including Data Landing Zones. This marks a significant shift in the company's data strategy.

Traditional Azure Data Platforms are now fading away, with Microsoft Fabric and Databricks emerging as the frontrunners. These platforms will be central to building data and analytics solutions within the Microsoft ecosystem.

While this shift has been gradually observed over the past two years, the recent official announcement provides a final call for architects and developers. It necessitates a complete re-evaluation of current platform design approaches.

The move consolidates Microsoft's offerings around more specialized and integrated solutions. Users are encouraged to adapt their strategies to leverage the capabilities of Databricks and Microsoft Fabric for future data initiatives.

Caption:
Usar IA 24/7 no tiene por qué significar quemar tokens sin control.

Como Data Engineer trabajando con IA, aprendí algo rápido:

El problema muchas veces no es el modelo.
Es cómo le pasas contexto, cómo estructuras el flujo y cuándo usas cada modelo.

Estos son hábitos que uso en mi día a día:

1. Resumir antes de enviar contexto.
2. Usar RAG solo con lo relevante.
3. Dividir tareas grandes en pasos pequeños.
4. Cachear lo repetido.
5. Usar modelos pequeños para tareas simples.
6. Medir coste por flujo, no por prompt.
Bien aplicado, es muy difícil reventar cuotas por puro desperdicio.

Optimizar tokens no es obsesionarse con ahorrar.
Es construir sistemas de IA más rápidos, más baratos y más robustos.

¿Qué haces tú para controlar tokens en producción?

Guarda este post para tu próximo proyecto con IA.
Compártelo con alguien que usa modelos grandes para todo.

Caption:
The coding mistake that is making your company’s data cleaning 100 times too slow. 🛑💻

Imagine having to reprint an entire 500-page textbook just because you needed to change a single sentence on page 20. It sounds ridiculous, but that is exactly how old data storage systems handle modifications. When an AI dataset needs to remove old, private, or incorrect information, it routinely rewrites massive files just to delete a few rows.

Upgrading to Apache Iceberg v3 introduces a feature called Deletion Vectors. Instead of rewriting the whole file, the system simply creates a tiny digital "sticky note" that tells the AI to skip specific lines. Deleting data literally becomes as fast as flipping a single switch.

But beware: if your processing software isn't updated to read these smart new notes, it will keep doing things the slow, expensive way.

Stop running outdated infrastructure. Learn to design elite corporate data streams at www.mcal.in.

Caption:
Pedi ao Gemini que criasse uma imagem para ilustrar um problema que eu estava enfrentando:

processar IA dentro da própria API.

Em um fluxo financeiro, isso acontece quando o usuário envia um relatório, uma nota de corretagem ou um extrato para leitura automática.

Se OCR, LLM, classificação e extração rodam dentro da requisição, a API fica presa esperando o processamento terminar.

Resultado: timeout, fila invisível, usuário travado na tela e risco de quebra quando mais arquivos entram ao mesmo tempo.

A solução é separar recebimento de processamento:

a API recebe o arquivo, cria um job_id e responde rápido.

O trabalho pesado vai para uma fila, é processado em background e o resultado pode ser consultado depois.

Na prática, isso transforma uma API frágil em uma arquitetura mais escalável para produção.

Já passaram por isso dentro do campo de trabalho de vocês?

  #InteligenciaArtificial #AutomacaoDeProcessos #ArquiteturaDeSoftware #FastAPI #DataEngineering
Author: pedropaulloazevedo

Caption:
Standing up real-time weather monitoring for 11 World Cup venues used to mean an afternoon of portal clicking — roughly 42 interactions per Eventstream source, multiplied by every city. The Fabric team just shrank that to a single English sentence.

A few things that stood out to me from this update:

One prompt described 11 RealTimeWeather sources, a humidity filter, and an Eventhouse destination — and the AI Skill deployed the whole topology in under 4 minutes via Fabric REST APIs.

RealTimeWeather is a zero-config source. No Azure subscription, no Event Hub provisioning, no connection credentials to manage.

Eventhouse Skills handle the consumption side too. Ask in English which venues are over the heat-stress threshold and it writes and runs the KQL for you.

The pattern generalizes — store environments, fleet telemetry, plant equipment, patient devices. Same cognitive effort whether you wire one source or eleven.

It also plugs into the tools devs already use: Copilot CLI, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf.

What multi-location monitoring scenario would you point this at first?

Iklan jalan terus. Budget habis terus. Tapi klik? Sepi. 👀

Kalau CPM tinggi dan CTR rendah, bisa jadi kamu cuma bayar supaya orang *lihat* — bukan supaya orang *klik*.

Masalahnya sering bukan di budget.

Masalahnya ada di:
❌ Target yang kurang tepat
❌ Kreatif yang kurang menarik
❌ Setup yang asal jalan
❌ Nggak paham cara baca data

Karena di Meta Ads, angka itu bukan sekadar angka.

Caption:
Kostenlose Sichtbarkeit in Bing – direkt in der KI-Antwort 🚀

Wir sprechen oft über Google, AI Overviews und KI-Sichtbarkeit.
Dabei lohnt sich auch ein Blick auf Bing.

Bei der Suchanfrage:
„SEO & GEO Agentur für Google & KI-Sichtbarkeit“
wird seosignals.de nicht nur in den Suchergebnissen angezeigt.

Die Website erscheint zusätzlich direkt innerhalb der hervorgehobenen KI-Antwort als Quelle.

Für mich zeigt das sehr deutlich, wohin sich Suchmaschinen entwickeln:
Früher ging es vor allem darum, auf Seite 1 zu stehen.

Heute geht es zunehmend darum, von Suchmaschinen und KI-Systemen verstanden, eingeordnet und als vertrauenswürdige Quelle genutzt zu werden.

Dazu gehören unter anderem:

✅ Klare Themenzuordnung
✅ Saubere technische Struktur
✅ Strukturierte Daten
✅ Eindeutige Inhalte
✅ Verständliche Entitäten und Zusammenhänge

Die eigentliche Sichtbarkeit entsteht nicht erst im Ranking.
Sie entsteht bereits dann, wenn Suchmaschinen beginnen, Inhalte zu verstehen und aktiv in ihre Antworten einzubauen.

Und genau diese Platzierung in der hervorgehobenen KI-Antwort kostet keinen Cent.

Die Reise geht weiter. 🚀

Caption:
Over the past week, you've been asking incredible questions about Spam Monitor. Let me answer the top 10 right here.

Q1: Does Spam Monitor work with all WordPress themes?
✅ Yes. It operates at the database and content level — completely theme-independent.

Q2: Will it slow down my website during scans?
✅ No. Scans run in the background using WordPress cron jobs. Zero impact on front-end performance.

Q3: Can it detect spam that was injected years ago?
✅ Yes. Spam Monitor scans ALL existing content — not just new content. Old injections are fully detectable.

Q4: Does it work with WooCommerce product pages?
✅ Absolutely. All WooCommerce custom post types are included in the scan.

Q5: What happens after it detects spam — does it auto-fix?
✅ Spam Monitor detects and alerts with precise location information. It guides you to fix — giving you full control over your content, never making automatic changes without your approval.

Q6: Is it suitable for non-technical users?
✅ 100%. The dashboard is designed for business owners, bloggers, and anyone — no technical knowledge required.

Q7: How is this different from a security plugin like Wordfence?
✅ Security plugins protect against active attacks and malware files. Spam Monitor specifically detects spam CONTENT — the SEO-destroying text, links, and patterns that security scanners completely miss.

Q8: Can agencies use it across multiple client sites?
✅ Yes. Multi-site support is part of the roadmap.

Q9: How often should I run scans?
✅ We recommend weekly automatic scans for active sites and monthly for low-traffic sites.

Q10: When is it launching?
✅ Very soon. Follow me to be notified the moment it goes live.

Any questions I missed? Drop it in the comments. 👇

#SpamMonitor #SEORepairKit #TorontoDigits #WordPress #FAQ #WordPressPlugin #SEO #WebsiteSecurity #DigitalMarketing

Caption:
Коли рекомендація блогера стає продажем? 🌟
 
Інфлюенсери впливають на покупки частіше, ніж здається. Але найчастіше вони не створюють потребу, а допомагають зробити вибір. 
 
👀 Найбільший вплив рекомендації мають тоді, коли людина вже замислювалася про покупку. Також важливо, щоб блогер сам давно та регулярно користувався продуктом. Для частини аудиторії додатковим стимулом стають спеціальні пропозиції та промокоди, а для інших – рекомендації в нових для себе категоріях.

І це не лише теорія.

📊 28% українців уже купували товари або послуги за рекомендацією блогера.
 
Втім, шлях до покупки не завжди прямий.  
 
Після реклами в контенті блогерів хтось просто переглядає інтеграцію, хтось переходить за посиланням із цікавості, а дехто зберігає інформацію, щоб повернутися до неї пізніше. Лише частина аудиторії одразу взаємодіє з брендом.
 
💡 Інфлюенсер-маркетинг працює не лише через миттєві продажі. Часто рекомендація стає ще одним аргументом, який допомагає споживачу впевнитися у своєму виборі.
Author: kantar_ukraine

Caption:
Рекомендації блогерів: що формує довіру? 👤🤳🏼 
 
На конференції IAB Connect 2026: «Диджитал і Конструктив» Клієнт-партнерка Kantar Україна Оля Капустян представила результати дослідження «Коли рекомендація стає продажем: як інфлюенсери та стрімери впливають на вибір українців». 
 
За даними Kantar, 79% українців сьогодні слідкують за контентом інфлюенсерів.  
Для багатьох це не лише розвага, а й спосіб дізнатися щось нове та орієнтуватися у новинках брендів. 
 
👀 Та чи довіряють українці рекомендаціям блогерів? 
Лише 17% українців довіряють рекламі інфлюенсерів, тоді як майже половина займає нейтральну позицію. 
 
🤝🏻 Найбільше на довіру впливають чесність, тривалий контакт з автором та відсутність надмірної реклами. 
 
Натомість вона швидко руйнується, коли реклама виглядає нещирою або створює відчуття «занадто ідеальної» картинки. 
 
💡 Для брендів це важливий сигнал: аудиторія очікує від інфлюенсерів не бездоганної реклами, а чесних і релевантних рекомендацій.
Author: kantar_ukraine

Caption:
One sentence from this week's newsletter has been stuck in my head.

The business is simply a tool. Your life is the destination.

I think a lot of aspiring entrepreneurs skip over that idea.

They spend hours researching business ideas, watching YouTube videos, listening to podcasts, and trying to figure out which opportunity has the most potential.

That matters, but what matters more is the kind of life you actually want.

A business can be profitable and still be a poor fit.

If you value flexibility, certain businesses will naturally fit better than others.

If your goal is building a large company, you'll make different decisions than someone who wants to stay small and work directly with clients.

If spending time with your family is one of your highest priorities, that should influence the type of business you build.

There isn't one version of entrepreneurial success.

There are thousands.

The important part is choosing yours before you start chasing someone else's.

That's the topic I explored in this week's edition of **Foundational Fridays** because I think it's one of the most overlooked parts of starting a business.

If you missed it, you can catch up using the link in the bio.

And if you're still trying to figure out what kind of business is right for you, I think you'll enjoy Foundational Fridays. Every Friday I share one practical lesson from my business school classroom to help you start smart.

 #entrepreneurship101 #entrepreneurshipclass #entrepreneur #businesseducation #startup #entrepreneurship #entrepreneurjourney #entrepreneurshiptip

Caption:
Microsoft, OpenAI’ya milyarlarca dolar yatırdı. Peki bu para nereye gidiyor ve geri dönüşü nasıl?

Çoğu kişi bunu filantropi veya saf teknoloji sevgisi sanır. Yanlış.

Bu, dijital dünyanın en büyük "kilitlenme" (vendor lock-in) stratejilerinden biri.

1. Altyapı Kiralama: OpenAI, modellerini eğitmek için devasa GPU gücüne ihtiyaç duyuyor. Microsoft, bu gücü Azure üzerinden veriyor.

2. Veri Akışı: OpenAI’nin her hesabı, Azure hesabıdır. Veri, Microsoft’un gözetiminden geçiyor.

3. Gelir Modeli: OpenAI, Azure kullanımı için kâr payı ödeyor. Yani OpenAI büyüdükçe, Microsoft’un cloud geliri katlanarak artıyor.

Özetle: Microsoft, OpenAI’yı satın almıyor. OpenAI’yı Microsoft’un en büyük "ürün satıcısı" haline getiriyor. 💸

Bu stratejiyi başka hangi alanda gördünüz?

Kendini geliştir, bilgiyi paylaş. 🔁

Caption:
From Research to Product

Building security tools taught me that finding vulnerabilities is only part of the challenge. The bigger challenge is helping different people understand and act on those findings.

Developers need technical details and remediation guidance. Leadership teams need to understand business risk and priorities. Compliance teams need evidence that aligns with frameworks such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and Cyber Essentials.

Too often, these audiences work from different tools, different reports, and different interpretations of the same issue. That observation became one of the foundations of VeriLync.

The goal is simple: transform security findings into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. This carousel shares some of the lessons and thinking that helped shape the product.

 VeriLync is launching soon!!!

If you’re building or scaling a SaaS business and looking for a more practical approach to application security and compliance readiness, join the waitlist for early access and launch discounts.

🌐 www.verilync.com
